title: Within-lap learning-to-rank sidecar
summary: Pit calls are competitive, not independent: strategists react to nearby rivals, and official F1 explanations emphasize undercut/overcut viability only when a rival is within striking range.
rationale: Pit calls are competitive, not independent: strategists react to nearby rivals, and official F1 explanations emphasize undercut/overcut viability only when a rival is within striking range. A query-group ranker over each `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` can learn who is most likely to pit next lap relative to the rest of the field, then be blended with the existing pointwise classifier.
implementation_hint: Train an XGBoost/LightGBM ranker with query id = `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` and label = `PitNextLap`. Use only leakage-safe current-lap features plus relative features already available or easy to add: percentile tyre age, percentile degradation, rank of lap delta within lap, position bucket, compound, stint, laps remaining, and nearest-position context. Blend rank scores with current binary model OOF predictions.
expected_effect: Should improve ordering among simultaneous pit candidates, especially in laps where 1-3 drivers are realistically in the window; likely modest standalone gain but useful ensemble diversity.
risk: If many groups have zero or multiple positives, rank training can be noisy; this needs race-blocked OOF blending rather than direct replacement.
